verdict_reasoning: B4GALT1 (UDP-Gal:betaGlcNAc beta-1,4-galactosyltransferase 1) is a type II membrane protein resident in the Golgi apparatus and trans-Golgi network. It possesses a single transmembrane domain near its N-terminus with a short luminal cytoplasmic tail and a large glycosyltransferase catalytic domain facing the lumen. However, B4GALT1 is well-documented to cycle between the Golgi and the plasma membrane as part of its normal trafficking and enzymatic function. It reaches the outer face of the PM via vesicular trafficking and can perform its galactosyltransferase function on glycoproteins at the cell surface. The protein contains a KKXX retrieval motif that mediates Golgi recycling, enabling steady-state surface cycling. This dual localization with documented surface activity qualifies it as surface-accessible via its own membrane-integrated mechanism.
